title: Chrysanthemum roxburghii
intro: Chrysanthemum roxburghii, also known as Ye Ju Hu or Yeju, is a traditional Chinese medicinal plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum roxburghii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, such as luteolin, quercetin, and rutin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum roxburghii is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support immune function and relieve respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum roxburghii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, a group of flowering plants commonly known as chrysanthemums or mums. The genus Chrysanthemum is further classified under the subfamily Anthemidoideae and the order Asterales. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants. Chrysanthemum roxburghii is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, where it is often found growing in the wild.
uses: Chrysanthemum roxburghii is used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia, including China and India. Its flowers and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. It is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health. In traditional medicine, Chrysanthemum roxburghii is often used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied and confirmed.
benefits: Chrysanthemum roxburghii has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its various health benefits. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is also said to exhibit antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects, which may help protect the liver from damage. Chrysanthemum roxburghii is also used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Additionally, it is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety.

These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, the plant is also rich in sesquiterpenoids, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The active constituents in Chrysanthemum roxburghii have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. parts: Chrysanthemum roxburghii, also known as the yellow chrysanthemum or Roxburgh's chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The flowers, leaves, and roots of the plant are used to create medicinal preparations. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. preparations: Chrysanthemum roxburghii is used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia, particularly in China and India. The plant is valued for its flowers, leaves, and roots, which are used to prepare herbal remedies. side_effects: Chrysanthemum roxburghii is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine in various Asian cultures. However, as with many plants used in traditional medicine, there is limited scientific research on its side effects.
